aboutPrivacyTabBlurb: By default your learning data stays in this browser. The sections below explain how GDPR applies to the app, public online trees (nostr://), forum and optional network sync, optional Sage AI, and what you can do.
aboutTreesysProductLine: Arborito is Treesys' first product: technology for humanity, starting with education
aboutCommunityGithubHint: Report bugs, suggest features, or contribute code. Arborito is free and open source (GPL).
certBody: This certifies that the student has successfully completed the module:
certTreeBody: This certifies that the student has successfully completed:
privacyAiBrowserLine: Optional. The browser does not download a local model: Sage GGUF chat is on the desktop app. On the web it only applies if you configure your own Expert API.
privacyAiDesktopLine: Optional, off by default. If you turn it on, the default model is {model} (~{mb} MB from Hugging Face). It runs entirely on your machine; the file stays local.
privacyDeviceDataLead: Delete everything Arborito stores in this browser (progress, settings, forum drafts, optional consents), or reset only optional consents for external lesson media, in-browser AI, and inline games. Data you already published on a public network tree is not removed from the network. Use the forum and construction tools for that.
privacyLegalBasisBody:
Local data (progress, settings, drafts): needed to provide the app you use (Art. 6(1)(b)).

Public network (Nostr relays, forum, nostr:// trees): only if you enable it. The basis is your explicit consent when accepting the privacy notice on the welcome screen before any WebSocket is opened (Art. 6(1)(a)). When some relays are located outside the EU/EEA (notably the United States), that same acceptance also works as the informed consent for the international transfer without an adequacy decision (Art. 49(1)(a)). We do not rely on «legitimate interest» (Art. 6(1)(f)) for sending your IP to non-EU relays: data-protection authorities reject that ground when there are no contractual safeguards with the operator, and Nostr, being a decentralised network, provides none.

Local AI (desktop), external media, inline games: explicit in-app consent, revocable in Profile (Art. 6(1)(a)).

Accessibility and voice (desktop): local voice download and the Sage microphone are optional; explicit consent when you turn them on (Art. 6(1)(a)). In the browser, read-aloud uses system speech with no download and no data sent to Arborito servers.

Legal obligation: only where applicable law requires it (Art. 6(1)(c)).
privacyLocalFirstCallout: Local-first: Your learning progress is stored in this browser (LocalStorage). By default it is not sent to a central Treesys database.
privacyNostrRelaysBody:
What they are. Public curricula linked with nostr:// are read and published over the Nostr network. Your browser opens secure WebSockets (wss://) to relays that act as meeting points for tree data and, where applicable, the forum.

Operators and privacy. Each relay is run by an independent third party. They may have their own legal terms and privacy notices; you should read the information published by the operator of the service you are using.

When it connects. If you accept the network during onboarding or configure relays in Privacy & data, the browser connects to the servers you chose (or the recommended bundle you confirmed). Each connection exposes your IP to the relay operator.

International transfers (GDPR Art. 49(1)(a)). Some relays operate outside the EU/EEA (notably in the United States), where the level of personal-data protection is not equivalent to the GDPR and we cannot sign Standard Contractual Clauses with each operator (Nostr is a decentralised network with no contract between client and relay). Before opening any WebSocket we therefore ask for your explicit, informed consent on the welcome screen (the «Accept» button). That consent is the specific legal basis under Article 49(1)(a) GDPR for occasional transfers to third countries without an adequacy decision: you accept that your IP and connection time will be visible to those operators in exchange for using the public features (forum, shared trees, ranking). If you do not accept, Arborito does not open any relay connection, the local-first mode keeps working without the network.

Withdrawing consent. You can withdraw your consent at any time from Privacy & data → «Reset optional consents». From that moment we will not open new connections; data that already left the browser cannot be retrieved from the Nostr network (no central operator controls it).

Changing relays. You can replace the default list from the advanced configuration (see docs/NETWORK.md in the repository). For instance, you can keep an EU-only set if your organisation requires it.

Already published content. Changing how your browser connects does not by itself erase information that was already spread on the network; availability or speed may vary depending on the network and operators.
privacyNetworkConsentGrantedBody: You accepted the privacy policy. Arborito can reach Nostr relays and the WebTorrent network on this device.
privacyNetworkConsentMissingBody: You have not accepted (or you withdrew) the privacy policy. Arborito will NOT contact any Nostr relay or WebTorrent peer until you accept.
nostrRelaysRequired: Enable the recommended network in Privacy & data (or save at least one relay) before registering.
syncLoginRelaysRequired: Enable the recommended network in Privacy & data (or save at least one relay) before registering.
privacyOnboardingNetworkPreviewBody: Online is not active yet. Accept and continue on the welcome screen will enable the recommended public network. Choose Continue offline (local only) to stay without network features.
privacyOnboardingNetworkPreviewSwitchAri: Online (enabled when you accept and continue)
privacyNetworkOnlineDisclaimer: Treesys publishes client-side software that can connect to decentralised networks after you accept the privacy policy in Privacy & data. We do not control, moderate, or host content transmitted through the relays you select. Responsibility for content and connectivity rests with you and the administrators of those external servers.
privacyResetConsentConfirmBody: We will clear saved choices for external lesson media, in-browser AI (Sage), local voice and microphone (desktop), the inline-game warning AND the network consent (Nostr / WebTorrent). Your learning progress and backups are not deleted, but Arborito will stop connecting to external networks until you accept again.
privacySecretsBody:
What is stored. When you sign in, Arborito keeps your online username, password, recovery key, and derived Nostr signing material in this browser's localStorage on this device, in plain text, alongside other settings. There is no central server backup and we never see these values.

What this means in practice. Anyone with access to this browser profile can copy them. Browser extensions can read localStorage. Clearing site data or using "Wipe local data" removes them permanently.

What we recommend. Export a sync key or set a recovery passphrase in Profile. To sign in on another device, use sync QR or import your sync key. Use "Forgot your password?" only if you forgot your password.

Your rights. These secrets never leave the device unless you export them. Profile → Delete all data on this device is the canonical erasure mechanism for this browser.
privacySecretsHeading: Identity keys stored on this device
privacyTechAnalytics: Analytics / ads: None in the app shell (no pixels, tag managers, or social embeds).
privacyTechCurriculum: Content data: Loaded only from sources you choose (local files or public nostr:// trees).
privacyTechFonts: Fonts: System fonts only (no Google Fonts or font CDNs).
privacyTechGraph: Styling / graph: Bundled CSS and custom canvas/SVG (no style CDNs).
privacyTechHosting: Hosting: Static web app (no server-side account for your lessons).
privacyText:
1. Data controller

{impressum}

2. Local storage (default)

Local-first: Arborito runs as a client-side application. By default, your progress, settings, and related data are stored locally in your browser (LocalStorage). We do not operate our own central database for your personal learning data.

3. GDPR & who is responsible

If you are in the EU/EEA or UK, the GDPR applies to personal data. For the Arborito software as published by Treesys, use the contact details in the Legal tab. For content and activity in a public online tree (a nostr:// link) or a tree loaded from elsewhere, the person or organisation that publishes or moderates that tree decides what is shared and may be the main contact for questions about that content.

Treesys provides open-source software; it does not review every community tree. Use the in-app controls to delete your posts, remove your online identity for a tree, export a backup file, delete all data on this device (Profile), or clear site data in your browser.

4. Public online trees, forum & sync

When you open a public tree, your browser may connect to peer-to-peer relays. Forum messages are cryptographically signed and readable by others with the same link. Optional learning progress sync is encrypted with keys stored on your device; the network stores ciphertext, not your plaintext progress.

Deletion and moderation use signed records so the app stops showing removed content in the canonical view. Copies may still exist on other devices or caches, this is a general limit of distributed systems.

5. Third-party services

Depending on how you use the app, network requests may reach:

  • Static hosting: Your browser loads the app HTML, scripts, and styles from whatever origin you use (for example a local server or your own deployment).
  • Nostr relays (nostr:// trees): Your browser opens secure WebSockets (wss://) to relays according to the Arborito installation or version you use. Operators may process typical TLS connection metadata and publish their own legal and privacy information. Forum traffic and public curriculum data follow the Nostr protocol.
  • WebTorrent (optional): some trees and the global directory may load over P2P. Your IP becomes visible to other peers in the swarm. If you would rather not expose it, avoid trees distributed via torrent; the rest keeps working over Nostr alone.
  • bundled runtime & Hugging Face (optional): On desktop, if you enable Sage and accept the download prompt: native llama.cpp binaries and model files are fetched from Hugging Face (and related CDNs for weights only). On web, Sage chat uses your own Expert API if configured, no in-browser model download.

No marketing stack: No Google Fonts, advertising pixels, tag managers, or social embeds ship with the app shell.

6. Your rights (GDPR)

Depending on context, you may have rights under Articles 15-22 GDPR (access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection). You can delete local data with Profile → Delete all data on this device (removes Arborito storage in this browser and reloads the app), or by clearing site data for this origin in your browser settings. You can reset optional consents for external media, in-browser AI, and inline games from Profile without deleting your progress. For data you published to a public tree, use the built-in tools (delete message, remove online identity) and contact the tree publisher if you need further help. In the EU, applicable law also allows lodging a complaint with the data protection authority in your country.
privacyWebTorrentBody:
What it is. Some trees and the global directory mirror can be distributed over WebTorrent, a peer-to-peer protocol where your browser exchanges data directly with other devices ("peers") that have the same content. This is what allows large curricula to scale without a central server.

Your IP address is visible to peers. By design, every peer in the swarm sees the IP address of every other peer (it is how the connection happens). This includes peers in any country. Your IP is also briefly visible to public WebRTC trackers used to discover peers. If you do not want your IP visible to strangers, avoid loading WebTorrent-distributed trees; trees that travel over Nostr only do not expose your IP this way.

VPN / Tor. A VPN masks your IP to peers as long as it is active; Tor does not work for WebTorrent's WebRTC transport. Your provider's terms still apply.

No central log. Arborito does not run a central swarm tracker and does not collect peer data. What you see is what the protocol exposes; we cannot delete IPs from other peers' memory after the fact.
privacyWindowsUpdateBody:
When. Only the Windows desktop installer (not the website, Flatpak, or Android APK) may contact GitHub Releases, and only after you accept the privacy policy (same gate as the public network). Until then, no update check runs.

What is sent. A version check exposes your IP and ordinary HTTPS metadata to GitHub, like opening a release page in a browser. Arborito does not send your lessons, progress, account, or identity with that check.

Download only after you agree. If an update exists, the app asks again. Only if you choose Update now does it download the installer and apply it quietly, then restart. Choosing Later skips the download until the next launch after privacy is still accepted.

Legal basis. The check is part of the network features covered by your privacy acceptance; the download itself proceeds only after the separate in-app update confirmation. Offline / local-only mode never contacts GitHub for updates.

profileBackpackDescCloudOn: Cloud sync is on; the file is an extra copy if you want one.
profileBackpackDescLocalOnly: Export a file before you clear the browser or switch devices.
profileImportReplaceBody: This replaces your current progress and profile on this device with the imported file. This cannot be undone. Continue?
profileLogoutClearsLocalConfirm: This signs you out and deletes Arborito local data from this browser.
profileModeLocalRisk: On the web, clearing site data can erase your progress.
syncLoginCreatedOk: Account ready. Save QR, code, or file, your choice.
syncLoginDeleteConfirm: Removes network sign-in for this name. Progress on this device stays; other devices stop syncing with this account.
syncLoginFileUnreadable: That file is not a valid secret export.
syncLoginNoAccount: No account found for that name.
syncLoginQrPairHint: Open Arborito on the other device, choose “Scan QR from my signed-in device”, and aim its camera at this QR.
syncLoginQrUnreadable: That QR is not a credentials code. Make sure the other device shows its access-key QR.
syncLoginRegisterForbiddenBody:
No relay accepted your account: every configured relay refused your key (typical messages: “blocked”, “restricted”, “not authorized”, “does not have permission”). Your name was NOT reserved on the network, so anyone else could still claim it. Change servers in Privacy & data or ask whoever maintains this Arborito copy to adjust DEFAULT_NOSTR_RELAYS (docs/NETWORK.md).

Technical detail: {detail}
syncLoginRegisterPublishFailedBody:
No relay accepted your account before the timeout. Your name was NOT reserved on the network, so anyone else could still claim it. Check your connection or change servers in Privacy & data and try again.

Technical detail: {detail}
syncLoginRenameConfirmBody: Only the name you type to sign in changes; the secret stays the same. Other devices use the new name when signing in.
syncLoginRotateOnlySync: Only sync-code accounts can rotate the secret here.
syncLoginRotateWarnBody: Old QR, text, and backup file stop working. Other devices must sign in again with the new set.
syncLoginRotatedOk: New secret generated. Old QR, code, and file no longer work.
syncLoginScanQrAim: Center the QR inside the green frame.
syncLoginScanQrLead: On your signed-in device open Profile → “Sync with QR”, then aim this camera at the code.
syncLoginScanQrUnsupported: This browser cannot scan QR codes. Use the form below with your username and password.
syncLoginSecretNotInSession: This session does not have your secret (for example after a full reload). Sign in again with your code or file, or generate a new secret below.
syncLoginShowQrCta: Show QR to pair a device
syncLoginUsernameTaken: That username is already taken. Pick another or sign in.
syncLoginUsernameTakenShort: That name is already taken. Try one of the suggestions below.
syncLoginSuggestionsLabel: Try one of these free names:
loginCredentialCodeRegisterHint: We will generate a secret code for you on the next screen.

profileGardenSoundHint: When you hit goals and care reminders
networkSocialConsentInfo: By creating an account you accept the Arborito privacy policy.

networkSocialConsentLead: Your online account publishes forum messages and, if you participate, your weekly score to third-party Nostr relays you chose (or the recommended bundle when you accepted the network). Each relay is run independently; your IP is visible when you connect. See Privacy for details. You can leave the ranking at any time.

recoveryPassphraseNote: Choose a phrase you will remember if you forget your password. Do not reuse your login password. The phrase never leaves this device.
recoveryEnterUsernameLead: Enter your username and the recovery passphrase you chose when you set it up.

profileOfflineNetworkBody: Without network consent there are no share codes, sync, forums, or online catalog. Accept the privacy policy in Privacy & data.
profileLimitedModeBody: Without network consent there are no share codes, sync, forums, or online catalog. Turn it on in Privacy & data.
profileNetworkModeAcceptHint: Accept the privacy policy below to enable the network.
profileNetworkModeOnHint: Third-party servers; Arborito does not host your courses.
profileNetworkModeOffHint: Offline: no share codes, sync, or online catalog.
profileDangerZoneHint: Permanent actions for your online account. Local progress on this device is not affected.
profileRegisterSpotlightBody: Your progress will be backed up encrypted and you can sign in on other devices with your password.
profileNetworkRelaysEmptyWarn: Without configured servers there are no share codes, sync, or online catalog. Restore the recommended bundle or add servers.
profileNetworkRelaysEditorHint: Operated by third parties; Arborito does not host your courses. You can add your own servers or restore the recommended bundle.
